hard.next_step: Prashant: to schedule a pre-demo with Kory in June
hard.ais_why: AI-product-led opportunity: Pattern wants to infuse ANA and intake into their Source-to-Pay layer for indirect, services, MRO/site spend, and supplier compliance workflows; Sr. Director Corporate Procurement leads the evaluation; sourced at NAPES with a June demo planned; budget not yet established.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early Qualified AI deal for Merlin Intake + ANA, single-threaded on one procurement director, with the June pre-demo committed to Kory Sosnowski never executed and no discovery, pain, or competitive read yet captured 117 days from the 31 Oct close.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 Jul 2026; close 31 Oct 2026 = 117 days out. In Qualified 39 days (created 27 May, qualified 28 May, still Qualified 6 Jul), zero submission-date milestones recorded. The enterprise motion (discovery, demos, RFI/RFP, shortlisting, workshops, commercials, negotiation, contracting) has not begun: no buyer call exists across 27 May - 6 Jul and the committed June pre-demo slipped. To be credible for 31 Oct the deal must run its first demo this week, complete discovery and multi-thread to an economic buyer by mid-August, and reach commercials by early September; current pace is well behind that line, but with 117 days the date is not yet lost.
evidence: Last Salesforce activity 24 Jun 2026 (12 days ago) was rep-side: Noopur Handa's Procurement AI World Tour invite to Kory Sosnowski, not a buyer-pulled next step
Zero Avoma buyer calls discovered across opp, account, and attendee-email discovery windows (27 May - 6 Jul 2026)
Next Step logged 18 Jun committed 'Prashant to schedule a pre-demo with Kory in June'; as of 6 Jul June has ended with no demo call on record, so the commitment slipped
Single-threaded: one Decision Maker contact role (Kory Sosnowski, Sr. Director Corporate Procurement and CoE); no economic buyer, IT, or finance stakeholder mapped after 39 days
Budget not established ('Budget - No', AIS_Why 28 May), so no funded path to the $100k / 31 Oct close

Products scoped to Merlin Intake + ANA (agentic AI procurement); AIS_Why names target use cases (indirect, services, MRO/site spend, supplier compliance) but no pain has been quantified and no integration or InfoSec scoping has started

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists after 39 days in Qualified. AIS_Why (28 May) states Pattern wants to infuse ANA and intake into their Source-to-Pay layer for indirect, services, MRO/site spend, and supplier compliance, which points at the automation and compliance pain but attaches no numbers: no intake cycle time, no headcount burden, no supplier price-variance figure, no savings target. With zero buyer calls there is no owner-stated impact to anchor urgency or justify the $100k. The move is to run pain-and-value discovery with Kory Sosnowski on the re-booked demo and quantify hours-per-intake, contract-review load, and supplier-compliance exposure.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. The only contact role is Kory Sosnowski, Sr. Director Corporate Procurement and Center of Excellence, marked Decision Maker and Primary (SF OpportunityContactRole) but a functional evaluation lead, not a confirmed budget owner. AIS_Why (28 May) states budget is not established ('Budget - No'), so the person who can fund a $100k purchase is unknown and unengaged. No call, email, or field names a VP Procurement, CFO, or CPO above Kory. The move is to ask Kory who owns and approves the budget for an ANA + intake purchase and secure an introduction before commercials.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Kory Sosnowski (Decision Maker, Primary)

decision_criteria.narrative: Partial signal from Salesforce, unconfirmed on a call. AIS_Why (28 May) says Pattern is 'looking for ANA + intake tool and building a requirement list', with the Sr. Director Corporate Procurement leading the evaluation, so the criteria are being written now and centre on ANA plus intake fit across indirect, services, MRO/site spend, and supplier compliance workflows. That is real evidence the criteria exist, but the actual weighting, must-haves, and any AI or integration requirement are not yet documented because no discovery or demo call has happened. The move is to obtain Pattern's requirement list and confirm the top three evaluation criteria on the next call.
sources: SF AIS_Why 28 May ('building a requirement list')

decision_process.narrative: The process is early and thinly evidenced. AIS_Why (28 May) shows Pattern was sourced at NAPES, the Sr. Director Corporate Procurement leads the evaluation, and a product demo was planned for June, which sketches a functional-lead-driven eval starting with a demo. But the June demo slipped (Next Step 18 Jun not executed), no RFI/RFP or shortlist step is on record, and the approval chain above Kory is unknown. Reading the call sequence as the process map is impossible because there is no call sequence yet. The move is to map the evaluation stages and approval steps with Kory and convert them into a shared timeline to 31 Oct.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unknown, which is expected at Qualified but must be surfaced early for an AI tool. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank, so there is no incumbent-contract forcing function behind the 31 Oct date. Nothing is on record about Pattern Energy's MSA standard, Legal redline posture, or an InfoSec review of an agentic AI system that would process supplier and contract data, and no SI or partner is named. For a utility handling supplier compliance, an InfoSec and data-handling review is a likely gate that can add weeks. The move is to ask Kory how procurement software is contracted and security-reviewed at Pattern so the paper path is visible before commercials.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is implied by the product intent but not yet confirmed by the buyer. AIS_Why (28 May) says Pattern wants to infuse ANA and intake into Source-to-Pay for indirect, services, MRO/site spend, and supplier compliance, which implies manual or fragmented intake routing, weak spend control on tail categories, and supplier-compliance overhead. That is a genuine directional signal, not invented, but no one at Pattern has articulated the pain, its owner, or its cost on a call because none has occurred. The pain owner is presumably Kory Sosnowski's procurement CoE. The move is to have Kory describe what is broken in intake and supplier compliance today and what happens if it is not fixed this year.

champion.narrative: No champion is activated. Kory Sosnowski (Sr. Director Corporate Procurement and CoE, Decision Maker, Primary) is the only contact and the natural champion candidate, but there is zero advocacy evidence: no internal introductions, no competitive intel shared, and the pre-demo he was to take in June did not happen (Next Step 18 Jun). His most recent engagement is accepting Noopur Handa's Bay Area AI World Tour invite on 24 Jun, which is interest but not championing. He is a potential champion, not yet developing. The move is to test him on the next call by asking for introductions to the budget owner and an IT/integration stakeholder; if he opens those doors he becomes a developing champion, if he gatekeeps we need a second thread.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Kory Sosnowski (Decision Maker), no Champion role
SF Task 24 Jun (AI World Tour invite accepted)

competition.narrative: The competitive field is completely dark, and on this deal that is a genuine knowledge gap rather than a sole-source conclusion. Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are blank, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c and Existing_vendor__c are blank, and with zero buyer calls no vendor, incumbent, or benchmark has ever been named. We cannot tell whether Pattern is running an uncontested build off a manual baseline or quietly benchmarking Merlin against Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, or a Source-to-Pay suite it already owns. The move is to ask Kory directly what they use for intake and supplier management today and which vendors are on their list, then position Merlin's agentic ANA + intake wedge against whatever surfaces.

deal_movement.summary: The deal has not advanced since it entered Qualified on 28 May: the June pre-demo committed 18 Jun slipped, and the only subsequent touch is a rep-side AI World Tour invite on 24 Jun. No buyer call has ever occurred.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is unknown, not sole-source. No competitor, incumbent, or current tool has surfaced in any field or call (Competitors__c, Existing_vendor__c, and How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c all blank; zero Avoma calls, 27 May - 6 Jul). We cannot yet tell whether Merlin is uncontested off a manual baseline or one of several vendors on Pattern's list. The single most urgent competitive action is to surface the current stack and the vendor shortlist from Kory on the first discovery/demo call.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Stated intent maps directly to our product scope: Pattern wants ANA + intake infused into Source-to-Pay, which is exactly Merlin Intake + ANA. Fit is aligned on paper but unvalidated because no demo or requirement review has happened.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified deal whose whole risk is momentum and coverage: no demo has happened, one contact, and the competitive and budget picture is blank. Win probability rises fastest by re-booking the demo, multi-threading to power, and leading with the agentic AI story.
flags: Re-establish cadence this week: the June pre-demo slipped, so putting a real buyer-facing demo on Kory Sosnowski's calendar is the highest-leverage lever now.
Multi-thread beyond Kory before commercials: map and reach the budget owner and an IT/integration stakeholder so the deal is not single-threaded on one director.

Lead with the Merlin ANA + intake agentic story against Kory's stated indirect/services/MRO and supplier-compliance use cases to build differentiation and urgency while the requirement list is still being written.

He is a potential champion, not yet developing, and the deal is single-threaded on him.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The opportunity is explicitly AI-led (Merlin Intake + ANA) and Pattern's stated intent is to infuse agentic AI into Source-to-Pay, but the story has not yet been put in front of the buyer because no demo has occurred. Under-positioned until the June demo is re-booked and the Merlin ANA narrative is landed against their use cases.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5.0, Status Moderate (SF): the deal is genuinely AI-product-led (buyer is seeking an ANA + intake tool), but excitement is unproven because budget is not established and no demo has validated the agentic AI value. No calls exist to over- or under-rate the field, so the AIS reads as fair.

An agentic AI intake/ANA tool for a utility will need ERP/procurement-stack integration and an InfoSec/data-handling review that have not been scoped and could add weeks.

confidence_signals.summary: Signals point to a cooling front-of-funnel: the committed June demo slipped, the only recent touch is a rep-side event invite, and there is no buyer-pulled next step. Not dark (last activity 24 Jun) but losing momentum.

win_position.text: Thin, early read: after 39 days in Qualified we hold a single Decision-Maker contact (Kory Sosnowski, Sr. Director Procurement) and have run no discovery or demo call, so pain, value, and the competitive field are all unproven (SF contact roles + AIS_Why, 28 May).
The pre-demo Prashant committed to schedule with Kory in June never landed, leaving the deal stalled at the front door with no buyer-facing event on the calendar (Next Step, 18 Jun).
Budget is explicitly not established ('Budget - No') and no economic buyer is mapped above Kory, so there is no funded path to the $100k / 31 Oct close (AIS_Why, 28 May).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is rep-push only: the last touch was Noopur Handa's Procurement AI World Tour invite to Kory on 24 Jun, not a buyer-pulled next step (Task, 24 Jun).
Zero buyer calls exist across 27 May - 6 Jul and the committed June demo slipped, so the deal has not moved past its Qualified entry (field history, 28 May).

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is unproven: no buyer has requested a meeting, named a timeline, or engaged commercially; Kory agreed to a pre-demo but did not drive it (Next Step, 18 Jun).

Genuine top-of-funnel intent exists: Pattern was sourced at NAPES, is actively building an ANA + intake requirement list, and Kory accepted the Bay Area AI World Tour invite (AIS_Why 28 May + Task 24 Jun).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded on one procurement director with no access to an economic buyer, IT, or finance; if Kory deprioritizes, nothing holds the deal up (SF contact roles, 27 May).
The competitive field is completely dark: no vendor, incumbent, or current stack is known, so we cannot tell if we are uncontested or one of several (SF Competitors__c blank).
